prose:
Method–provenance summary  
Property: Condensed Fukui functions (f+ electrophilic, f– nucleophilic, dual descriptor) for phenol (13 atoms).  
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with **exact four-center integrals** (density fitting OFF) in PySCF 2.x.  
Geometry provenance: single-point charges on the user-supplied phenol.xyz (no re-optimization); the N, N+1, and N– states were computed at the same fixed geometry (DFT//DFT).  
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).  
Electronic state: charge 0, multiplicity 1 (singlet parent); cation and anion states were computed as doublets (charge ±1, mult 2).  
Software/backend: chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill → PySCF.  
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles converged for all three charge states.  The engine issued three WARNINGS (verbatim):  
• "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"  
• "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"  
• "common header present"  
Integrity verdict: trustworthy = true.  
Live log: the run streamed to a `fukui_<timestamp>.out` file (printed when the job started); result JSON saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpv32gk8gi/live.json and bar-chart PNG at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/argo_o3/20260704-201458_phenol_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_02_plot.png.
